upper.title: Modal Verbs: Permission, Obligation, Prohibition, Necessity
upper.content:
📌 Permission – Can, could, may, might
Dùng để xin phép, cho phép hoặc từ chối.

Xin phép (informal): Can I sit here?
Xin phép (formal): Could/Might/May I use this chair?
Cho phép (informal): You can sit here.
Cho phép (formal): You may use this chair.
Từ chối (informal): Sorry, you can't use this chair.
Từ chối (formal): Sorry, you may not use this chair.

⚠️ Trong quá khứ dùng was/were allowed to hoặc phủ định couldn't — KHÔNG dùng could ở khẳng định:
• He wasn't allowed to sit down during the lesson.
• He was allowed to receive visits. ✅ (NOT He could receive visits.)
📌 Obligation – Must / Have to
Must = nghĩa vụ cá nhân (từ người nói):
• I must give up smoking. (Tôi tự thấy cần)
Have to = nghĩa vụ bên ngoài (quy định, người khác yêu cầu):
• I have to give up smoking. (Bác sĩ bảo vậy)

Cũng có thể dùng 've got to (informal):
• I've got to be there before ten.

Trong quá khứ: dùng had to trong mọi trường hợp:
• I had to leave early.
📌 Prohibition & Absence of Obligation – Mustn't / Don't have to
Mustn't = CẤM (bắt buộc KHÔNG làm):
• You mustn't reveal where you get the information.
Don't have to = KHÔNG BẮT BUỘC (có thể làm nhưng không cần):
• You don't have to arrive before 7.
📌 Necessity – Need to, needn't, didn't need to, needn't have
Need to / have to = cần thiết:
• We need to confirm our reservations before Friday.
Don't need to / needn't = không cần thiết:
• Tell him he needn't wash the dishes. I'll do it later. (một lần cụ thể)
• The doctor said I don't need to wear glasses. (nói chung)

Didn't need to / needn't have + past participle — khi việc không cần thiết nhưng đã làm:
• You didn't need to buy / needn't have bought anything. Thank you!
Didn't need to — khi việc không cần thiết và không làm:
• I didn't need to buy groceries. Sarah told me.
📌 Be allowed to / Be permitted to / Be supposed/meant to / Had better
Be able to / be allowed to thay cho can:
• We were allowed to eat all we wanted.
Be (not) permitted to = cấm hoặc cho phép (chính thức/quy định):
• It is not permitted to take photos.
• Picnics are not permitted in the park.
Be supposed/meant to = đáng lẽ phải / không được phép:
• We are supposed to check in one hour before take-off.
• What are you doing? You aren't supposed to be here.
Had better / 'd better not = nên làm (có hậu quả tiêu cực nếu không):
• You 'd better not be late or you'll miss the flight.
You mustn't tell anyone about this — it's strictly confidential. But you don't have to attend the meeting if you're busy.Mustn't (cấm) vs Don't have to (không bắt buộc)
We needn't have prepared so much food — half of the guests cancelled at the last minute.Needn't have + past participle: đã làm nhưng không cần thiết

exercises.q: You ___ use this product without protection — it's dangerous.
exercises.exp: Mustn't = cấm (prohibition) — việc sử dụng không có bảo hộ bị cấm vì nguy hiểm

exercises.q: You ___ go to the ceremony if you don't feel like it. It's optional.
exercises.exp: Don't have to = không bắt buộc — buổi lễ không phải bắt buộc tham dự

exercises.q: We ___ made that decision. It was a huge mistake in hindsight.
exercises.exp: Shouldn't have + past participle = đáng lẽ không nên làm (chỉ trích hành động quá khứ)

exercises.q: He ___ take the bus because his brother picked him up at the station.
exercises.exp: Didn't need to = không cần thiết VÀ không làm — anh ấy không đi xe buýt

exercises.q: The refugees ___ work outside the camp. It's against the rules.
exercises.opts: are not allowed to,mustn't,don't have to,needn't
exercises.exp: Are not allowed to = không được phép (quy định bên ngoài áp đặt)

exercises.q: We couldn't find a hotel so we ___ sleep in the car. It was awful!
exercises.opts: had to,must have,should have,were supposed to
exercises.exp: Had to = bắt buộc trong quá khứ (hoàn cảnh bắt buộc)

exercises.q: You ___ forget to take your medicine. It's absolutely essential.
exercises.exp: Mustn't = cấm quên (obligation not to forget — very important)

exercises.q: We ___ get up early because it was a holiday, so we slept till noon.
exercises.exp: Didn't need to = không cần thiết và không làm — họ không dậy sớm

exercises.q: You ___ call me as soon as you arrived. I was so worried about you!
exercises.opts: were supposed to,must,had better,are permitted to
exercises.exp: Were supposed to = đáng lẽ phải làm (nghĩa vụ không được thực hiện)

exercises.q: ___ I use your phone for a moment? Mine has run out of battery.
exercises.exp: Could = xin phép lịch sự (formal permission request)

exercises.q: You ___ spend so much money last summer. Now you have nothing left.
exercises.opts: shouldn't have,needn't have,mustn't have,didn't need to
exercises.exp: Shouldn't have + past participle = đáng lẽ không nên làm (chỉ trích)

exercises.q: Employees ___ use the staff entrance. Visitors must use the main door.
exercises.opts: are permitted to,must to,are supposed not to,needn't to
exercises.exp: Are permitted to = được phép (quy định chính thức)

exercises.q: You ___ be late for the exam — you'll miss the first section.
exercises.exp: 'd better not = không nên (có hậu quả tiêu cực nếu vi phạm)

exercises.q: It ___ take photos inside the museum. Please put your camera away.
exercises.opts: is not permitted to,mustn't,is not allowed to,doesn't have to
exercises.exp: It is not permitted to = không được phép (cấu trúc chính thức với 'it')

exercises.q: She realised she ___ have brought a gift — it was a casual dinner.
exercises.exp: Needn't have + past participle = đã mang quà nhưng thực ra không cần

exercises.q: I ___ speak to the manager. My complaint was ignored three times.
exercises.opts: need to,had better,must to,am supposed not to
exercises.exp: Need to = cần thiết phải làm (tình huống đòi hỏi hành động)

exercises.q: Back then, students ___ stand up whenever the teacher entered the room.
exercises.opts: had to,must,were needed to,are supposed to
exercises.exp: Had to = bắt buộc trong quá khứ (quy tắc trường học)

exercises.q: You ___ bring anything — just yourself. We have plenty of food.
exercises.opts: don't have to,mustn't,needn't have,aren't allowed to
exercises.exp: Don't have to = không bắt buộc mang gì (không cần thiết)

exercises.q: All delegates ___ register at the front desk upon arrival. No exceptions.
exercises.opts: are to,are able to,had better,are supposed not to
exercises.exp: Are to + infinitive = quy định/mệnh lệnh chính thức

exercises.q: We ___ have waited so long — there was a shortcut we didn't know about.
exercises.exp: Needn't have + past participle = đã chờ nhưng thực ra không cần

exercises.q: You ___ (not/permit) to enter this area without a security badge.
exercises.exp: Be not permitted to = không được phép (chính thức)

exercises.q: She ___ (not/supposed) work on Sundays, but she often does anyway.
exercises.exp: Be not supposed to = không đáng lẽ phải làm theo quy định

exercises.q: I ___ (have to) apologise to him. It was entirely my fault.
exercises.exp: Had to = bắt buộc trong quá khứ

exercises.q: You ___ (better/not) tell her the secret — she'll tell everyone.
exercises.exp: 'd better not = lời khuyên mạnh có hậu quả xấu nếu làm

exercises.q: We ___ (not/need) book in advance — the restaurant was empty.
exercises.ans: didn't need to book / needn't have booked
exercises.exp: Didn't need to / needn't have + past participle: không cần đặt trước

exercises.q: Children under 12 ___ (not/allow) to watch this film without an adult.
exercises.exp: Are not allowed to = không được phép (quy định)

exercises.q: You ___ (should/not) have lied to me. I feel really hurt now.
exercises.exp: Shouldn't have + past participle = đáng lẽ không nên nói dối

exercises.q: All staff ___ (be/report) to the main office by 9 a.m. tomorrow.
exercises.exp: Are to + infinitive = chỉ thị chính thức

exercises.q: I ___ (need/not) stay late tonight — the report is already finished.
exercises.exp: Don't need to / needn't = không cần thiết (lần này cụ thể)

exercises.q: He realised he ___ (must/leave) his umbrella on the bus.
exercises.exp: Must have + past participle = suy luận chắc chắn về quá khứ

exercises.q: Students ___ (not/allow) to use their phones during the examination.
exercises.exp: Are not allowed to = quy định cấm dùng điện thoại

exercises.q: You ___ (ought/study) harder if you want to pass this exam.
exercises.exp: Ought to = lời khuyên/nghĩa vụ đạo đức

exercises.q: She ___ (suppose) call me, but I haven't heard from her all day.
exercises.exp: Was supposed to = đáng lẽ phải làm (nhưng chưa làm)

exercises.q: You ___ (may/not) park here between 8 a.m. and 6 p.m.
exercises.exp: May not = không được phép (chính thức/formal)

exercises.q: I ___ (have/got) finish this assignment tonight. The deadline is tomorrow.
exercises.exp: Have got to = bắt buộc phải làm (informal, như must)

exercises.q: The teacher said students ___ (not/supposed/write) in the margins of the textbook.
exercises.exp: Aren't supposed to = không được phép theo quy định

exercises.q: She ___ (must/leave) the meeting early yesterday. It was mandatory for all staff.
exercises.exp: Had to = bắt buộc trong quá khứ (quy định bắt buộc)

exercises.q: You ___ ('d better/not/go) out without your umbrella — the forecast says heavy rain.
exercises.exp: 'd better not = không nên (khuyến cáo có hậu quả)

exercises.q: He ___ (not/need/pay) for the tickets. They were complimentary for guests.
exercises.ans: didn't need to pay / needn't pay
exercises.exp: Didn't need to = không cần thiết (không phải trả tiền)

exercises.q: Travellers ___ (not/permit) bring more than 100ml of liquid through security.
exercises.exp: Are not permitted to = không được phép (quy định an ninh)

exercises.q: I ___ (should/not/eat) so much chocolate last night. Now I feel terrible.
exercises.exp: Shouldn't have + past participle = đáng lẽ không nên làm (chỉ trích)

exercises.q: All delegates ___ (be/arrive) at the conference room by 8:30 a.m. sharp.
exercises.exp: Are to + infinitive = chỉ thị chính thức

reorder.words: you mustn't tell anyone about the results before Friday
we had to wait for two hours before they let us in
she was not allowed to take her phone into the exam room
you don't have to come if you don't feel well
all passengers are to fasten their seatbelts before takeoff
we needn't have prepared so much food for the party
you had better not be late for your interview tomorrow
visitors are not permitted to take photographs in this area
he was supposed to send the report by Monday but he forgot
I don't need to renew my passport until next year
she shouldn't have spent all her savings on that holiday
employees are not meant to use the private car park
you are not supposed to enter without knocking first
he needn't have bought flowers but it was a lovely gesture
students are required to wear uniforms at all times on campus
you ought to apologise to her before it is too late
we were not allowed to leave the building during the fire alarm
she didn't need to explain because everyone already understood
you must not exceed the maximum speed limit on this road
I had better call the doctor if the pain gets any worse

Visitors {{4}} light fires anywhere along the route — this {{5}} due to the high risk of wildfires in the area. However, hikers {{6}} book their visit in advance, as the trail operates on a drop-in basis. writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một nơi công cộng (bảo tàng, bệnh viện, sân bay…) và mô tả các quy định ở đó — những gì được phép, bị cấm, và bắt buộc phải làm — dùng đa dạng các modal verbs.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Must, mustn't, be allowed to, be permitted to, don't have to, have to, are to
writing_prompts.example: When visiting the National Art Museum, there are several rules all visitors must follow. Photography is not permitted inside the galleries, and visitors are not allowed to touch any of the exhibits. Food and drinks must not be brought beyond the entrance hall. However, visitors don't have to book tickets in advance — they can simply arrive and purchase them at the door. Children are to be accompanied by an adult at all times, and large bags are to be left in the cloakroom. Despite the restrictions, visitors are allowed to sketch the artworks and are encouraged to ask staff questions. The museum also states that guided tours must not be interrupted, as other guests are paying for the experience.

writing_prompts.vi: Nhớ lại một tình huống trong quá khứ khi bạn hoặc ai đó đã làm điều không cần thiết, đáng lẽ không nên làm, hoặc bị bắt buộc phải làm điều gì đó. Dùng các modal verbs về quá khứ.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Shouldn't have, needn't have, didn't need to, had to, was/were allowed to, was supposed to
writing_prompts.example: Last year, I had to take a last-minute flight to attend a family emergency abroad. I needn't have panicked so much — my colleague had already handled most of the arrangements. However, I shouldn't have rushed to the airport without double-checking my documents, because I arrived to find that I wasn't allowed to board without a visa, which I had completely forgotten to arrange. I was supposed to have applied for it weeks earlier. In the end, I didn't need to rebook for the next day — a kind staff member found a workaround. Still, I should have been more organised, and I certainly needn't have wasted so much energy panicking when the solution was straightforward.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về các quy định ở trường học hoặc nơi làm việc — ngày xưa và bây giờ khác nhau như thế nào? Những gì được phép hoặc bắt buộc đã thay đổi ra sao?
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Had to, weren't allowed to, are permitted to, don't have to, are supposed to, ought to
writing_prompts.example: When my father was at school in the 1970s, students had to stand up every time the headmaster entered the classroom. They weren't allowed to speak unless directly addressed, and they had to wear a strict uniform even during the summer months. Corporal punishment was permitted, which would be completely unacceptable today. Nowadays, students don't have to stand for teachers, and they are allowed to express their opinions in class discussions. However, they are still supposed to arrive on time and must not use their phones during lessons. Some educators argue that pupils today ought to be held to higher standards of discipline, while others believe the changes have created a healthier learning environment.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết thư hoặc email phàn nàn về một quy định mà bạn cho là bất công hoặc không cần thiết. Giải thích tại sao quy định đó không nên tồn tại.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Mustn't, are not allowed to, ought to, should, don't need to, have to
writing_prompts.example: Dear Manager, I am writing to express my concerns about the new office policy requiring all employees to request permission before working from home. In my view, staff ought to be trusted to manage their own schedules without having to submit formal requests three days in advance. We are not supposed to be treated as if we cannot make responsible decisions independently. Most of my colleagues don't need to come into the office every day to do their jobs effectively — in fact, many report being more productive at home. I strongly feel that this policy should be reviewed. Employees ought not to have to justify every remote working day. I hope you will consider revising the rules so that they are fairer and more in line with modern working practices.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về một tình huống bạn hoặc ai đó được phép làm điều gì đó đặc biệt, hoặc không được phép và cảm thấy thế nào. Dùng cả hình thức quá khứ và hiện tại.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Was/were allowed to, couldn't, was permitted to, needn't have, had to, are now allowed to
writing_prompts.example: When I was sixteen, I was not allowed to stay out past nine o'clock in the evening. My parents had strict rules, and I was supposed to call home if I was ever going to be even five minutes late. I couldn't attend parties unless a parent had spoken directly to the host beforehand. At the time, I felt these restrictions were deeply unfair. However, looking back, I think I ought to have been more understanding — my parents were simply worried. Now that I have my own children, I realise they had better know where they are at all times, and I understand why some boundaries are necessary. My son is now allowed to go out alone in the evenings, which I couldn't do at his age, and I think that is a reasonable change.

upper.title: Speculation and Deduction: Modal Verbs and Expressions
upper.content:
📌 Must – Suy luận chắc chắn (Certain deduction)
Dùng must + infinitive khi khá chắc chắn điều gì đó là sự thật ở hiện tại:
• They are almost identical. They must be sisters.
• You must be kidding!

Dùng must be + -ing cho hành động đang xảy ra:
• Can you hear the shouting? Our neighbours must be arguing again.

Dùng must have + past participle hoặc must have been + -ing cho quá khứ:
• I can't find my wallet! I must have dropped it in the taxi.
• He must have been sleeping when the fire started.
📌 Can't – Suy luận phủ định chắc chắn (Negative deduction)
Dùng can't + infinitive khi khá chắc điều gì đó KHÔNG đúng:
• He can't be that famous. I've never heard of him.
• They can't be travelling by bus. There's no bus service today.

Dùng can't/couldn't have + past participle cho quá khứ:
• You can't/couldn't have seen John. He was in hospital.

⚠️ KHÔNG dùng mustn't để suy luận phủ định:
❌ He mustn't be famous. → ✅ He can't be famous.
📌 Could/Might/May – Khả năng (Possibility)
Dùng could/might/may + infinitive khi điều gì đó có thể là sự thật:
• He could/might/may be away.
• Tom isn't at the office. He might be working from home.

Dùng could/might/may have + past participle cho khả năng trong quá khứ:
• They could/might/may have finished by now.
• She might have been having a shower.

⚠️ Phủ định khả năng: dùng might not / may not (KHÔNG dùng could not):
• He might not be aware of the risks.

Could have cũng dùng cho điều có thể xảy ra nhưng không xảy ra:
• He could have played in the first division but had a bad injury.
📌 Should/Ought to – Dự kiến xảy ra (Expected situation)
Dùng should/ought to + infinitive cho tình huống dự kiến ở hiện tại/tương lai:
• He should/ought to be home by now. He's always back before 7.

Dùng should have/ought to have + past participle cho tình huống dự kiến trong quá khứ (hoặc chỉ trích):
• He should have received the parcel yesterday. I sent it two days ago.
📌 Bound/Sure, Likely/Unlikely, Definitely/Probably
Be bound/sure to = gần như chắc chắn:
• He is bound/sure to win the final.
Be likely/unlikely to = có/không có khả năng xảy ra:
• He is likely to win. / It is unlikely that she will leave.
Definitely (trước will/won't hoặc sau subject) = chắc chắn:
• She will definitely pass. / He definitely won't agree.
Probably (trước will, sau will not) = có lẽ:
• She will probably call. / He probably won't come.
The lights are off and the car isn't in the driveway. They must have gone out for the evening.Must have + past participle: suy luận chắc chắn về quá khứ
She scored 100% in every test this year. She is bound to win the scholarship.Be bound to: gần như chắc chắn xảy ra

exercises.q: I fell running yesterday and I think I ___ my ankle. It's very swollen.
exercises.opts: might have sprained,must sprain,will sprain,should have sprained
exercises.exp: Might have + past participle: khả năng đã xảy ra trong quá khứ (không chắc chắn)

exercises.q: He ___ win the competition. He's the best by far — no one comes close.
exercises.exp: Is bound to = gần như chắc chắn sẽ xảy ra

exercises.q: The light in Tom's room is on. He ___ computer games in there.
exercises.opts: must be playing,must play,must have played,could play
exercises.exp: Must be + -ing: suy luận về hành động đang xảy ra lúc này

exercises.q: He ___ German very well. He's only lived in Germany for a month.
exercises.opts: can't speak,mustn't speak,isn't bound to speak,won't definitely speak
exercises.exp: Can't speak = suy luận phủ định: chắc chắn anh ta không giỏi tiếng Đức

exercises.q: The burglar broke in at noon. Some neighbours ___ him from their windows.
exercises.opts: might have seen,will definitely see,must see,should see
exercises.exp: Might have seen = khả năng đã nhìn thấy trong quá khứ (không chắc)

exercises.q: I ___ a lot as an accountant, but the work was unbearably dull so I quit.
exercises.opts: could have earned,have definitely earned,must have earned,should earn
exercises.exp: Could have earned = điều có thể xảy ra nhưng không xảy ra

exercises.q: This track ___ the next big hit of the year. It's already going viral.
exercises.opts: is sure to be,must have been,will unlikely be,is probably not
exercises.exp: Is sure to be = gần như chắc chắn sẽ xảy ra

exercises.q: He ___ go to prison. He has a clean record and cooperated fully with police.
exercises.exp: Is unlikely to = không có khả năng xảy ra

exercises.q: 'Where's the cat?' 'Have you checked under the sofa? She ___ there.'
exercises.opts: may be hiding,might hide,could have been,must hide
exercises.exp: May be hiding = khả năng đang xảy ra lúc này (hành động tiếp diễn)

exercises.q: You ___ a copy of the contract by email the day after you signed it.
exercises.opts: should have received,must be bound to receive,will unlikely receive,can't have received
exercises.exp: Should have received = dự kiến đã xảy ra (nhưng có thể không)

exercises.q: She's been studying medicine for six years. She ___ know the basics of anatomy.
exercises.exp: Must = suy luận chắc chắn: 6 năm học thì chắc chắn biết rồi

exercises.q: I can't find my keys anywhere. I ___ them in the office when I left.
exercises.opts: must have left,might leave,should leave,could leave
exercises.exp: Must have left = suy luận chắc chắn về hành động quá khứ

exercises.q: She looked exhausted. She ___ very hard all week for the presentation.
exercises.opts: must have been working,must work,might work,should have worked
exercises.exp: Must have been working = suy luận về hành động kéo dài trong quá khứ

exercises.q: He ___ passed the test. He told me himself that he didn't open the books once.
exercises.opts: can't have,mustn't have,shouldn't have,might not have
exercises.exp: Can't have + past participle = chắc chắn điều gì KHÔNG xảy ra trong quá khứ

exercises.q: It's nine o'clock. He ___ home by now — he always finishes work at six.
exercises.opts: should be,must be,might have been,can't be
exercises.exp: Should be = tình huống được dự kiến (expected situation) ở hiện tại

exercises.q: She didn't answer the door last night. She ___ asleep when I knocked.
exercises.opts: might have been,must not be,can't have been,should be
exercises.exp: Might have been = khả năng đã xảy ra trong quá khứ (không chắc)

exercises.q: The results ___ announced tomorrow. All the staff are waiting nervously.
exercises.opts: will probably be,must be,can't be,might not have been
exercises.exp: Will probably be = dự đoán có khả năng cao (probably + will)

exercises.q: He's been living abroad for twenty years. He ___ homesick sometimes.
exercises.opts: must get,can't get,won't definitely,might not have
exercises.exp: Must get = suy luận: hợp lý khi cho rằng anh ta nhớ nhà

exercises.q: Look at the queue! There ___ hundreds of people waiting to get in.
exercises.opts: must be,might have been,can't be,should have been
exercises.exp: Must be = suy luận mạnh dựa vào bằng chứng trước mắt

exercises.q: He ___ travelled by boat. He's absolutely terrified of water.
exercises.exp: Can't have + past participle = chắc chắn KHÔNG làm điều đó trong quá khứ

exercises.q: She ___ (must/practise) for months — her performance was absolutely flawless.
exercises.exp: Must have been + -ing: suy luận về hành động kéo dài trong quá khứ

exercises.q: The house is dark and the car is gone. They ___ (might/go) on holiday.
exercises.exp: Might have + past participle: khả năng đã xảy ra

exercises.q: He ___ (can't/be) the thief — he was with us the whole time.
exercises.exp: Can't have been = chắc chắn không phải (phủ định suy luận quá khứ)

exercises.q: The traffic is terrible. We ___ (likely/arrive) late for the meeting.
exercises.exp: Be likely to = có khả năng xảy ra

exercises.q: She ___ (should/receive) the package by now — it was sent three days ago.
exercises.exp: Should have + past participle: tình huống dự kiến đã xảy ra

exercises.q: I can hear someone in the kitchen. It ___ (must/be) my flatmate cooking dinner.
exercises.exp: Must be = suy luận chắc chắn ở thì hiện tại

exercises.q: You look exhausted. You ___ (must/sleep) very badly last night.
exercises.exp: Must have + past participle: suy luận về quá khứ dựa vào bằng chứng

exercises.q: He ___ (bound/regret) that decision one day. It's a huge mistake.
exercises.exp: Be bound to = gần như chắc chắn xảy ra trong tương lai

exercises.q: She ___ (might/not/know) about the changes yet — nobody called her.
exercises.exp: Might not = khả năng phủ định (không dùng could not)

exercises.q: He ___ (could/win) the championship if he hadn't got injured that season.
exercises.exp: Could have + past participle: điều có thể xảy ra nhưng không xảy ra

exercises.q: Those footprints are very large. They ___ (can't/belong) to a child.
exercises.exp: Can't = chắc chắn không đúng (suy luận phủ định hiện tại)

exercises.q: She ___ (definitely/pass) — she's the most prepared student in the class.
exercises.exp: Definitely + will: chắc chắn sẽ xảy ra

exercises.q: The roads are icy. The journey ___ (likely/take) much longer than usual.
exercises.exp: Be likely to = có khả năng xảy ra

exercises.q: They ___ (ought to/arrive) hours ago. Something must have gone wrong.
exercises.exp: Ought to have + past participle: tình huống dự kiến (và có thể chỉ trích)

exercises.q: She's smiling at her phone. She ___ (must/get) good news just now.
exercises.ans: must have got / must have gotten
exercises.exp: Must have + past participle: suy luận chắc chắn về quá khứ gần

exercises.q: The traffic is completely stopped. There ___ (must/be) an accident ahead.
exercises.exp: Must be = suy luận chắc chắn dựa vào bằng chứng trực tiếp

exercises.q: He looks exhausted. He ___ (must/work) very hard recently with all the deadlines.
exercises.exp: Must have been working = suy luận về giai đoạn kéo dài trong quá khứ gần

exercises.q: The library is silent. There ___ (might/not/be) anyone here today.
exercises.exp: Might not be = khả năng phủ định (may not be)

exercises.q: She ___ (bound/forget) to submit the form. It's extremely important to her.
exercises.ans: is bound to forget / is sure to forget
exercises.exp: Is bound to = gần như chắc chắn (nhưng có thể quên)

exercises.q: The results ___ (should/receive) by now. The email was sent two days ago.
exercises.exp: Should have been = dự kiến đã xảy ra nhưng có thể không

exercises.q: He ___ (can't/be) the CEO of the company. He's only twenty-five years old.
exercises.exp: Can't be = suy luận phủ định chắc chắn

exercises.q: The concert ___ (likely/sell out) quickly. It's very popular in this city.
exercises.exp: Is likely to = có khả năng xảy ra

exercises.q: It ___ (seem) that the project is behind schedule. We need to speed up.
exercises.exp: It seems (that) = có vẻ như/có vẻ là

exercises.q: She ___ (could/win) the scholarship if she had applied on time.
exercises.exp: Could have + past participle = điều có thể xảy ra nhưng không xảy ra

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả một tình huống bí ẩn — ai đó đột ngột biến mất, một căn phòng bị bỏ trống, hoặc một sự kiện kỳ lạ. Suy luận về những gì có thể đã xảy ra bằng cách dùng modal verbs suy luận.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Must have, can't have, might have, could have, must be, may be
writing_prompts.example: I arrived at the café to find my friend's coat and bag on the table but no sign of her anywhere. She must have stepped outside for a moment, perhaps to take a call, but her phone was still on the table, so that can't have been the reason. She might have gone to the bathroom, but I had already checked. Her coffee was still warm, which meant she must have been there very recently — no more than a few minutes before I arrived. Her wallet was open on the table, which was strange. She can't have left willingly without her things. I thought she might have seen someone she knew and gone to speak to them. Or she could have felt unwell suddenly. I must have missed something obvious, but I couldn't work out what had happened. I was beginning to think something must have gone wrong.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết đoạn văn suy đoán về tương lai — điều gì có thể, chắc chắn, hoặc khó có thể xảy ra trong 20 năm nữa (công nghệ, môi trường, xã hội).
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Is likely to, is bound to, is unlikely to, might, will probably, is sure to, could
writing_prompts.example: In twenty years, technology is bound to have transformed daily life beyond recognition. Artificial intelligence is likely to handle most routine administrative tasks, which means millions of jobs as we know them today will probably no longer exist. Electric vehicles are sure to dominate the roads, and cities are likely to have redesigned their infrastructure to accommodate them. However, some challenges are unlikely to disappear so easily — inequality and access to healthcare might still be serious global problems. Space tourism could become commercially available for the wealthy, though it is unlikely to be affordable for most people within this timeframe. Meanwhile, renewable energy is bound to become the dominant power source globally, which might finally slow the rate of climate change. Whatever happens, the world in 2045 is sure to look dramatically different from today.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả một người bạn vừa gặp hoặc quan sát — suy đoán về nghề nghiệp, tính cách, cuộc sống của họ dựa vào ngoại hình và hành vi của họ.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Must be, might be, can't be, could be, is likely to, seems to, must have
writing_prompts.example: The woman sitting across from me on the train is fascinating to observe. She must be in her mid-forties, though she could be younger — she has a very energetic way of moving. She's reading what looks like a legal document, so she might be a lawyer or a consultant of some kind. She can't be a teacher — she doesn't have the patient, slightly tired look I associate with the profession. She's wearing an expensive watch, so she must have a well-paid job. She keeps checking her phone with a slightly anxious expression, which suggests she might be expecting important news. She looks as though she's been travelling for several hours — there are bags under her eyes. She is likely to be heading to a business meeting in the city. Whoever she is, she seems to be someone who carries a great deal of responsibility.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về một kết quả hoặc thi đấu quan trọng sắp diễn ra — ai likely to win, unlikely to succeed, bound to struggle. Phân tích khả năng của các bên.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Is bound to, is likely to, is unlikely to, is sure to, might, could, will probably, can't
writing_prompts.example: The championship final this weekend is likely to be one of the most closely contested matches of the decade. The defending champions are bound to start as favourites — they have been in exceptional form all season and have not lost a single home game. However, the challengers are unlikely to be intimidated. Their striker is sure to be a threat, having scored in every round of the tournament. The match might be decided by individual moments of brilliance rather than team tactics. The home crowd could prove to be a significant factor, as they will probably create an intense atmosphere. However, if the challengers defend solidly in the first half, they might be able to catch the home side on the counter-attack. Whatever happens, one thing is certain: the losing team can't be happy with the result, regardless of how hard they fight.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả bằng chứng tại một hiện trường (crime scene, tai nạn, hoặc sự kiện bất thường) và đưa ra suy luận về những gì đã xảy ra.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Must have, can't have, might have, could have, must have been, ought to have
writing_prompts.example: The investigators arrived at the scene and tried to piece together what must have happened. A window on the ground floor had been broken from the outside, so the intruder can't have had a key. They must have known the house would be empty — there was no sign of hesitation in the entry point. Several drawers had been opened and searched methodically, which meant the person must have been looking for something specific rather than taking valuables at random. A glass of water on the kitchen counter was still cold, so the break-in must have occurred within the last hour. The alarm had not been triggered, which was strange — the intruder might have known the code, or could have disabled it somehow. The neighbours ought to have heard something, but no one had reported anything unusual. The investigators concluded that the intruder must have been someone familiar with the property.

upper.title: Verbs of the Senses
upper.content:
📌 Stative uses – Feel, hear, see, smell, taste (không dùng continuous)
Khi các động từ feel, hear, see, smell, taste diễn tả ấn tượng qua 5 giác quan, chúng là stative verbs — KHÔNG dùng ở dạng tiếp diễn. Thay vào đó, dùng can/can't cho hành động tại thời điểm nói:

• I can see a woman near your car. ✅ (NOT I am seeing)
• Sorry, I can't hear you. Can you speak up? ✅
• I can smell petrol. Have you filled up? ✅
• I can't taste the flavour. I've burned my tongue. ✅
• I can feel your heartbeat. ✅
📌 Dynamic uses – Khi là hành động chủ động (dùng được continuous)
Khi feel, look, smell, taste là hành động chủ ý (voluntary), có thể dùng dạng tiếp diễn:

• Why are you smelling the milk? Is it off? (cố tình ngửi)
• He's tasting the soup to check the salt. (nếm thử)
• I'm feeling the fabric to see if it's soft. (sờ thử)
• Why are you looking in that direction? (nhìn chủ động)

Feel cũng dùng được dạng tiếp diễn để nói về cảm giác sức khoẻ/cảm xúc:
• 'How are you feeling?' 'I'm not feeling very well.'
📌 See và hear với nghĩa khác (dynamic)
See với nghĩa gặp gỡ, hẹn hò:
• I'm seeing Jerry for lunch. (=gặp)
• We've been seeing each other for months. (=hẹn hò)
Hear với nghĩa nhận thông tin:
• I've been hearing strange things about him.
📌 Sense verbs + infinitive hoặc gerund
Sau feel, hear, see, watch, notice, listen dùng:
Gerund (-ing) = hành động đang diễn ra (chưa kết thúc), lặp lại:
• I saw them kissing in the park. (đang diễn ra, không thấy kết thúc)

Bare infinitive = hành động hoàn chỉnh từ đầu đến cuối, thường ngắn:
• I saw them kiss in the park. (thấy toàn bộ, nhanh)
📌 Sense verbs as linking verbs + adjective/like/as if
Look, smell, taste, sound, feel + adjective:
• When you use that cream, your skin feels really smooth.
• He sounded nervous on the phone.
+ like + noun (tương tự như):
• You sound like your mother. / This tastes like chicken.
⚠️ Smell/taste of = mùi/vị của vật thật có trong đó;
Smell/taste like = mùi/vị giống như:
• The soup smells of garlic. (có tỏi thật) vs The soup smells like garlic. (tương tự mùi tỏi)
+ as if/as though + clause:
• You sound as if you had a long party last night.
Seem, appear, look + adjective / + to-infinitive:
• She seems tired. / He seems to have a natural gift for music.
• It appears that they won't help us.
I could smell something burning in the kitchen, and when I went in I saw the chef tasting something straight from the pot.Can smell (stative) vs tasting (dynamic – deliberate action)
She sounds as if she has been crying — her voice feels very strained and distant.Sounds as if + clause; feels + adjective (linking verb)

exercises.q: I ___ the cake when my mother came in and asked if it tasted good.
exercises.exp: Was tasting (dynamic – deliberate action: nếm thử xem bánh có ngon không)

exercises.q: I'm not feeling well, and it's so cold I ___ my toes at all.
exercises.opts: can't feel,am not feeling,don't feel,haven't felt
exercises.exp: Can't feel (stative – perception through touch at this moment)

exercises.q: Sorry, there's a lot of noise and I ___ you very well. Can you speak up?
exercises.opts: can't hear,am not hearing,don't hear,haven't heard
exercises.exp: Can't hear (stative – perception at this moment)

exercises.q: 'Why ___ the sofa like that?' 'I think he can smell something unusual.'
exercises.opts: is the dog smelling,does the dog smell,has the dog smelt,did the dog smell
exercises.exp: Is smelling (dynamic – deliberate action: chủ ý ngửi để kiểm tra)

exercises.q: 'Where's James?' 'He's just left, but I ___ him tomorrow at seven. Message?'
exercises.exp: Am seeing (dynamic – meaning 'meeting someone': hẹn gặp)

exercises.q: It ___ cheese in here. Did someone leave the fridge open?
exercises.exp: Smells of = mùi của thứ thực sự có ở đó (there is actual cheese)

exercises.q: He ___ he was not very fond of the idea when I mentioned it.
exercises.opts: sounded as if,sounded if,sounded of,sounded like he was
exercises.exp: Sounded as if + clause (linking verb + as if)

exercises.q: It ___ that we've got lost. None of these streets look familiar.
exercises.exp: It seems (that) + clause: nối với mệnh đề

exercises.q: She ___ to have a real natural gift for languages. She learns so fast.
exercises.exp: Seems to + infinitive: seem + to-infinitive (ngầm hiểu dựa vào bằng chứng)

exercises.q: I ___ at her while speaking to you and she looks absolutely fantastic.
exercises.exp: Am looking (dynamic – deliberate action: đang chủ động nhìn)

exercises.q: Why ___ the milk? Do you think it might be off?
exercises.opts: are you smelling,do you smell,have you smelt,did you smell
exercises.exp: Are you smelling (dynamic – deliberate: putting your nose near to check)

exercises.q: I ___ someone calling my name from across the street.
exercises.exp: Could hear (stative perception at a past moment)

exercises.q: This sauce ___ exactly like the one my grandmother used to make.
exercises.exp: Tastes (stative linking verb: không dùng continuous)

exercises.q: I watched the children ___ in the playground for a while.
exercises.exp: Playing (gerund after watch: hành động đang diễn ra, không thấy kết thúc)

exercises.q: I heard the door ___ shut at exactly midnight. It woke me up.
exercises.exp: Slam (bare infinitive: hành động hoàn chỉnh ngắn, thấy từ đầu đến cuối)

exercises.q: She ___ as though she hadn't slept properly in days.
exercises.exp: Looks as though + clause (linking verb + as though)

exercises.q: You ___ very confident today. Is something good happening?
exercises.exp: Sound (stative linking verb: không dùng continuous với nghĩa này)

exercises.q: I ___ the fabric carefully before deciding whether to buy it.
exercises.exp: Was feeling (dynamic – deliberate: sờ thử vải để kiểm tra chất liệu)

exercises.q: He ___ a doctor about his symptoms. He looks quite unwell.
exercises.opts: should see,should be seeing,should look,must feel
exercises.exp: Should see (with dynamic meaning 'consult/visit')

exercises.q: This perfume ___ roses with a hint of something citrus underneath.
exercises.opts: smells like,smells of,is smelling like,tastes like
exercises.exp: Smells like = the smell is similar to roses (không nhất thiết có hoa thật)

exercises.q: I ___ (can/smell) something burning. Is there a problem in the kitchen?
exercises.exp: Can smell = stative perception ở thì hiện tại

exercises.q: Why ___ (you/taste) the soup? Does it need more salt?
exercises.exp: Are you tasting = dynamic: deliberate action (nếm thử chủ ý)

exercises.q: She ___ (seem/be) extremely knowledgeable about art history.
exercises.exp: Seem + to-infinitive: diễn tả nhận định dựa trên quan sát

exercises.q: He was ___ (taste) the new recipe to see if it needed more seasoning in the sauce.

exercises.q: I ___ (feel) the temperature dropping — it might snow later tonight.

exercises.q: She ___ (see) her optician next Tuesday for a routine check-up of her eyesight.
exercises.exp: Is seeing = dynamic meaning 'meeting/appointment' (gặp bác sĩ)

exercises.q: The old house ___ (smell/of) damp and something sweet — maybe jasmine in the garden.
exercises.exp: Smells of = có mùi của thứ thực sự có mặt (damp + jasmine thật)

exercises.q: He ___ (was/listen) to the radio all morning while working on his research report.
exercises.exp: Was listening = dynamic: action diễn ra trong quá khứ (chủ ý theo dõi)

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả một nơi đặc biệt bạn đã đến — một nhà hàng, khu chợ, khu vườn, rừng, bãi biển — chú trọng đến tất cả các giác quan: nhìn, nghe, ngửi, nếm, cảm giác.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Stative and dynamic sense verbs, can + sense verb, linking verbs + adjective
writing_prompts.example: The morning market in the old quarter of Hanoi was unlike anything I had ever experienced. The moment I stepped inside, I could smell freshly cut herbs and spices — the air tasted faintly of lemongrass and something sharp I couldn't identify. Around me, vendors were shouting and the noise sounded as if every stall was competing for attention simultaneously. I could see enormous piles of tropical fruit, their colours almost impossibly bright in the early light. A woman beside me was feeling the surface of a mango carefully before buying it. One vendor handed me a small piece of pineapple to try — it tasted of pure sweetness with a slightly acidic edge. The ground felt slippery beneath my feet from the water used to wash the vegetables. Everything looked alive and chaotic, yet strangely organised. It seemed like a world entirely its own, completely separate from the quiet streets outside.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể lại một cuộc gặp gỡ với người lạ — mô tả người đó trông như thế nào, giọng họ ra sao, cảm giác của bạn trong cuộc trò chuyện.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Look + adjective, sound + as if/like, seem to, feel + adjective, appear to
writing_prompts.example: I met her quite by chance in a bookshop on a rainy Tuesday afternoon. She looked to be in her sixties, though she moved with an energy that seemed younger. Her hair looked as though she had never quite bothered to organise it properly, and her coat felt expensive when it brushed against my arm as she reached past me for a book. When she spoke, her voice sounded warm and unhurried, as if she had nowhere else to be. She seemed to know a great deal about the authors on that particular shelf. I could hear genuine passion in every sentence she said. The conversation felt surprisingly natural for two strangers, and I noticed her smiling at something I said. By the time we parted, I appeared to have spent nearly an hour talking to someone whose name I had never asked. She looked like someone who had lived a very interesting life.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một sự kiện gây ra bởi giác quan — nghe thấy một âm thanh lạ, ngửi thấy mùi khói, cảm thấy điều gì đó không đúng. Dùng cả stative và dynamic uses.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Could hear, could smell, could feel, was listening, was looking, felt like, sounded as if
writing_prompts.example: It was nearly midnight when I first noticed something was wrong. I could smell smoke — not the gentle smell of a candle, but something sharper and more urgent. I sat up and listened carefully. The house was quiet, but I could hear a faint crackling sound somewhere below me. I went to the top of the stairs and looked down. I couldn't see any flames, but the air felt unusually warm. The smoke was getting thicker and it smelt of burning plastic, which was alarming. I could feel my heart beating faster. I knocked on my flatmate's door — she sounded confused when she answered, as if she had been deeply asleep. Within a minute, we were both standing outside in the cold, watching the kitchen fill with smoke through the window. The whole experience felt like something from a nightmare, and my hands were still shaking an hour later.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết bài review về một nhà hàng hoặc quán cà phê — mô tả không khí, thức ăn, âm thanh và tổng thể cảm nhận.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Taste, smell, look, sound, feel as linking verbs + adjective/like/as if
writing_prompts.example: We visited La Fontaine last Saturday evening and came away thoroughly impressed. The restaurant looked elegant but not intimidating — the lighting was warm and the tables felt well-spaced without being cold. When we arrived, the room sounded lively but not uncomfortably loud. Our starter, a mushroom risotto, smelt of truffle and fresh herbs and tasted incredibly rich. My main course looked almost too beautiful to eat — the presentation was extraordinary. The sauce tasted of slow-cooked wine and something subtly smoky that I couldn't quite identify. The bread felt fresh and still slightly warm from the oven. Our server seemed genuinely knowledgeable about the menu and sounded enthusiastic without being overbearing. The dessert tasted like a childhood memory of chocolate — deep, comforting, and completely indulgent. The whole evening felt like a genuine treat. It seemed like the kind of place that takes real pride in what it does.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể lại một kỷ niệm tuổi thơ liên quan đến giác quan — mùi, âm thanh, vị, hình ảnh gắn với một người hoặc nơi chốn đặc biệt.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Could smell, could hear, tasted of, smelt like, looked, seemed, felt like
writing_prompts.example: Some of my clearest childhood memories are built around the senses rather than events. My grandmother's kitchen always smelt of cinnamon and something floral — I think it was the soap she used. The moment I walked in, the whole house felt different from anywhere else: warmer, slower, as if time moved at a different pace. I could hear her humming to herself while she cooked, a sound that seemed to belong entirely to that place. The food she made tasted of pure familiarity — her apple cake tasted like comfort itself, slightly sharp and sweet at the same time. I can still feel the rough texture of the old kitchen table under my hands. When I look back, everything seemed brighter in those memories, as if the world looked more vivid then. Even now, certain smells — warm bread, lavender soap — bring it all back completely and without warning. It seems like a different life, but it felt like the whole world.

upper.title: Get: Different Meanings
upper.content:
📌 Get + direct object = obtain, receive, buy, bring, catch
Obtain (đạt được, lấy được):
• He got his driving licence last week.
Receive (nhận):
• I got a letter from John yesterday.
Buy (mua):
• Can you go to the shop to get some milk?
Bring (mang lại, lấy giúp):
• Can you get me a couple of glasses from the kitchen?
Catch (bắt xe/phương tiện):
• Do you think we'll get the 7.40 train?
📌 Get + to + place = arrive, reach (đến nơi)
• He always gets home very late.
• We got to London on the 8 o'clock train.
• How long does it take you to get to work?
📌 Get + adjective/past participle = become (trở nên, bị)
Get + adjective (trở nên):
• I'm getting old.
• He gets angry very easily.
• You should go. It's getting dark.
Get + past participle (trở nên trong trạng thái đó):
• I'm getting worried. / We got married last year.
📌 Get = be (passive) – Bị làm điều gì
• I got caught by the police. (=was caught)
• She almost got killed by a drunk driver.
• His articles are always getting criticised. (=being criticised)
📌 Get something done = have something done (nhờ/thuê ai làm)
• We are getting the house painted next week.
• You should get your tyres changed.
• I need to get my hair cut.
📌 Get someone to do something = persuade/make someone do something
• How can parents get their children to read more?
• I can't get him to listen to me.
• She finally got the mechanic to look at the car for free.
I need to get my phone repaired — the screen is cracked — and I also need to get to the service centre before it closes.Get + object + past participle (have done) vs get to + place (arrive)
It's getting late and I can't get her to answer her phone. I'm starting to get worried.Get + adjective (become) and get someone to do (persuade)

exercises.exp: Get = receive (get + direct object = receive)

exercises.exp: Getting cold = becoming cold (get + adjective = become)

exercises.q: We need to ___ the roof fixed before winter arrives. It's leaking badly.

exercises.q: I need to ___ to the bank before it closes at four. Can we leave now?

exercises.exp: Got him to admit = persuaded him to admit

exercises.q: How long does it take you to ___ work every morning from your flat?
exercises.exp: Get to work = arrive at work (get to + place)

exercises.q: I need to ___ my eyes tested. I've been getting headaches from reading.
exercises.exp: Get + object + past participle = arrange for something to be done

exercises.q: She ___ (get/angry) when people are late. It's one of her biggest pet hates.
exercises.exp: Gets angry = becomes angry (get + adjective = become)

exercises.q: Can you ___ (get) me those files from the cabinet in the meeting room?
exercises.exp: Get me = bring/fetch me (get + indirect object)

exercises.q: We finally ___ (get/to) the hotel at midnight after the flight was delayed twice.
exercises.exp: Got to = arrived at (get to + place)

exercises.q: He ___ (get/his car/service) before the long road trip next week.
exercises.ans: is getting his car serviced / needs to get his car serviced
exercises.exp: Get + object + past participle = have something done

exercises.q: I just don't ___ (get) the point of this exercise. Can you explain it again?
exercises.exp: Get = understand (informal)

exercises.q: She ___ (get/hurt) when she fell off her bike but luckily nothing was broken.
exercises.exp: Got hurt = was hurt (get + past participle = passive)

exercises.q: It ___ (get/late). We should probably think about heading back home.
exercises.exp: Is getting late = becoming late (get + adjective)

exercises.q: Could you ___ (get) some bread and milk on your way home from work?
exercises.exp: Get = buy (get + direct object = buy)

exercises.q: I can't ___ (get/her/understand) why this is so important to us.
exercises.exp: Get someone to do = make/persuade someone to do something

exercises.q: He ___ (get/stop) by the police twice on his way to the concert last night.
exercises.exp: Got stopped = was stopped (get + past participle = passive)

exercises.q: We need to ___ (get/the heating/fix) before winter or it'll be freezing.
exercises.exp: Get + object + past participle = arrange for it to be done

exercises.q: I ___ (get/a promotion) last month and I'm really excited about the new role.
exercises.exp: Got a promotion = received/obtained; got promoted = was promoted

exercises.q: She couldn't ___ (get/to) the station in time and missed the last train home.
exercises.exp: Get to = arrive at (reach the station in time)

exercises.q: He ___ (get/sack) after the company discovered he had been leaking information.
exercises.exp: Got sacked = was sacked (get + past participle = passive)

exercises.q: We finally ___ (get/to) the summit after climbing for eight hours non-stop.
exercises.exp: Got to = arrived at/reached (get to + place)

exercises.q: She ___ (get/bad) results in her exams and felt completely devastated about it.
exercises.exp: Got bad = received/obtained (get + direct object)

exercises.q: I can't ___ (get/this machine/work) no matter what I try. It's completely broken.
exercises.exp: Get something to work = make something function

exercises.q: He ___ (get/promote) to senior manager last month after five years of hard work.
exercises.exp: Got promoted = was promoted (get + past participle = passive)

exercises.q: Can you ___ (get) some stamps from the post office on your way back home?
exercises.exp: Get = buy/obtain (get + direct object = obtain)

exercises.q: It's ___ (get/dark) early in winter. We should finish before 5 p.m. today.
exercises.exp: Getting dark = becoming dark (get + adjective = become)

exercises.q: She finally ___ (get/him/admit) that he had made a mistake in the calculations.
exercises.exp: Got him to admit = persuaded him to admit (get + object + to-infinitive)

exercises.q: He ___ (get/reject) three times before his novel was finally accepted for publication.
exercises.exp: Got rejected = was rejected (get + past participle = passive)

exercises.q: I need to ___ (get/my hair/cut) before the job interview next week.
exercises.exp: Get something done = have something done (arranged)

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về một lần mọi thứ đều không suôn sẻ trong một ngày — giao thông, đồ đạc hỏng, người không hợp tác. Dùng các nghĩa khác nhau của 'get'.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Get + adjective, get to + place, get something done, get someone to do, got + past participle
writing_prompts.example: Last Monday was, without question, the most chaotic day I have had in years. I was trying to get to an important meeting across town, but the traffic was getting worse by the minute due to an unexpected road closure. I got stuck behind a lorry for forty minutes and arrived at the office nearly an hour late. My laptop had stopped working the night before, and I hadn't managed to get it repaired in time, which meant I had to borrow a colleague's machine. I couldn't get the presentation file to open properly on their computer, so I had to improvise. I was getting more stressed by the second. At lunch, I got my wallet stolen on the bus, which was the final blow. By the time I got home that evening, I was completely exhausted. I got the locks changed anyway, as a precaution, and vowed to have a better week from that point onwards.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả quá trình bạn đạt được một mục tiêu hoặc thành tích quan trọng — bạn đã phải thuyết phục ai, nhờ ai giúp, hoặc trải qua những thay đổi nào để đạt được nó.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Get a job/degree/award, get someone to do, get something done, get to + place, get + adjective
writing_prompts.example: Getting my first article published was not an easy process. I had been trying for almost a year to get an editor to take my work seriously. I got several rejections in the early months, which was disheartening, but I kept going. I got my writing reviewed by a mentor who pointed out some structural weaknesses. I couldn't always get her to give me detailed feedback — she was very busy — but what she did say was invaluable. Gradually, I was getting better and more confident in my voice. I got to a point where I felt the work was genuinely strong. I sent the piece to a well-known online publication and, weeks later, got a message saying they wanted to publish it. When I finally saw it live, I couldn't quite get my head around it at first. Getting that first piece out there opened many doors, and I am still grateful for everyone who helped me get to where I am now.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về kinh nghiệm cải tạo nhà, mua xe, hoặc sắp xếp một sự kiện lớn — sử dụng 'get something done', 'get someone to do', và 'get to'.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Get + object + past participle, get someone to do, get to + place, get + adjective
writing_prompts.example: Organising my parents' anniversary party was far more complicated than I had anticipated. The venue we had originally chosen fell through, so I had to get an alternative sorted within a week. I couldn't get the original caterer to refund the deposit, which was frustrating, and it took several phone calls to get a new company to agree to our budget. I got a friend who designs interiors to help with the decorations, which saved us a great deal of time. Getting all the RSVPs confirmed was another challenge — I couldn't get some relatives to respond at all, no matter how many messages I sent. By the morning of the event, I was getting quite anxious, but once everything was set up, it all looked wonderful. We got a local band to play in the evening, which the guests loved. By the end of the night, I had got through what felt like the most logistically demanding day of my life, but seeing my parents so happy made every stressful moment completely worthwhile.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về lần đầu sống một mình hoặc trong môi trường mới — những thay đổi bạn đã trải qua, những vấn đề bạn phải giải quyết.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Get used to, get + adjective, get to + place, get something done, get someone to
writing_prompts.example: When I first moved to the city on my own, I found it overwhelming. Getting to know the area took weeks — I got lost almost every time I went out, and navigating public transport was a constant challenge. Setting up the flat was another battle: I couldn't get the internet connected for the first ten days, and I had to get a neighbour to help me assemble the furniture because I didn't have the right tools. I got my mail redirected, set up my bills, and slowly got everything organised. At first, I was getting quite homesick, but things started to improve. I got to know some colleagues and began exploring the city at weekends. Getting used to cooking for myself was probably the biggest adjustment — I had to get some basic skills and couldn't always get the results I hoped for. But gradually, I was getting more confident and independent. Looking back, moving away was the best decision I ever made.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một trải nghiệm du lịch khi mọi thứ không diễn ra như kế hoạch — chuyến bay, hành lý, khách sạn, và cách bạn giải quyết.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Get to + place, get + past participle, get something done, get someone to, getting + adjective
writing_prompts.example: Our trip to Portugal last spring did not go according to plan. We couldn't get to the airport on time because of a motorway accident, and by the time we arrived, we had missed our original flight. Getting rebooked on the next available flight took three hours, and we got charged an additional fee that we later managed to get refunded after a long complaint process. When we finally got to Lisbon, we discovered that our luggage had been sent to a different city. We couldn't get the airline's customer service team to give us a clear answer about when it would arrive. For the first two days, we had to get some basic essentials from local shops. Getting everything resolved was exhausting and we were getting quite stressed. However, once the bags arrived and we had settled into the hotel, the holiday itself was wonderful. We got to explore parts of the city that weren't in any guidebook, and in the end, we got more out of the trip than we had originally planned.
